claim: Muse Spark comes in instant mode and thinking mode, with a rumored deep think/contemplating mode coming soon
actionable: When using Muse Spark, select thinking mode for complex reasoning tasks and instant mode for speed-critical operations
source_title: Muse Spark - Meta's NEW Llama Replacement

claim: Muse Spark performed poorly on benchmarks including Scale AI's own HELM exam with tools, coming in last among tested models
actionable: Avoid using Muse Spark for humanities reasoning and tool use tasks; prefer Claude Opus or Gemini for those domains
source_title: Muse Spark - Meta's NEW Llama Replacement

claim: Meta spent 14 billion dollars acquiring Scale AI's Alexander Wang to build Meta Superintelligence Labs for competitive advantage
actionable: Recognize that massive spending on researcher talent alone does not guarantee model quality; results matter more than investment
source_title: Muse Spark - Meta's NEW Llama Replacement

claim: Muse Spark is proprietary, not open source, with no API access available and future open source versions only promised
actionable: Do not plan to deploy Muse Spark as a base for open source applications unless and until an open weights version is released
source_title: Muse Spark - Meta's NEW Llama Replacement

claim: Third-party benchmarks show Muse Spark sits in top five models, ahead of Claude Sonnet but behind Gemini, GPT-5.4, and Claude Opus
actionable: For mid-tier reasoning tasks, consider Muse Spark as competitive alternative to Claude Sonnet if Muse Spark API becomes available
source_title: Muse Spark - Meta's NEW Llama Replacement
